report_name: AI-Powered Cyberattacks Are Overwhelming Critical Infrastructure Defenses, Experts Warn

identities.name: Critical Infrastructure Operators
identities.description: Operators of critical infrastructure such as hospitals, utilities, power grids, and transportation systems are being targeted by AI-driven cyberattacks due to the convergence of their IT and OT networks.

indicators.name: Anomalous IT to OT Network Traffic
indicators.pattern:
title: Anomalous IT to OT Network Traffic
id: 2b1a9b8f-3d4c-4e5f-8a1b-9c8d7e6f5a4b
status: experimental
description: |
  Detects potentially anomalous network traffic from an IT network segment to an OT (Operational Technology) network segment.
  This rule is a template and requires customization of the source and destination IP ranges for IT and OT zones.
references:
  - https://industrialcyber.co/news/ai-powered-cyber-threats-overwhelm-human-defenders-forcing-critical-infrastructure-operators-toward-automated-security/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-19
tags:
  - attack.t0886
level: medium
logsource:
  category: firewall
detection:
  selection:
    src_ip: '192.168.1.0/24' # Placeholder for IT Zone
    dst_ip: '10.10.0.0/16'   # Placeholder for OT Zone
    dst_port:
      - 135
      - 139
      - 445 # SMB/RPC
      - 3389 # RDP
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects anomalous traffic patterns between IT and OT network segments, such as the use of IT-specific protocols attempting to communicate with devices in the OT environment. This could indicate lateral movement from a compromised IT system.

indicators.name: Potential Unauthorized PLC Logic Modification
indicators.pattern:
title: Potential Unauthorized PLC Logic Modification
id: 5e8f1a2b-3c4d-4e5f-8a1b-9c8d7e6f5a4c
status: experimental
description: |
  Detects modification of common PLC (Programmable Logic Controller) project files on engineering workstations.
  Unauthorized changes to these files could indicate an adversary attempting to manipulate physical control processes.
references:
  - https://industrialcyber.co/news/ai-powered-cyber-threats-overwhelm-human-defenders-forcing-critical-infrastructure-operators-toward-automated-security/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-19
tags:
  - attack.t0840
level: high
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    TargetFilename|endswith:
      - '.ACD'  # Rockwell/Allen-Bradley
      - '.ap16' # Siemens STEP 7
      - '.pro'  # Schneider Electric
      - '.prz'  # Omron
  filter_legit_process:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\RsLogix.exe'
      - '\Studio5000.exe'
      - '\S7.exe'
      - '\UnityPro.exe'
  condition: selection and not filter_legit_process
indicators.description: Detects unauthorized changes to Programmable Logic Controller (PLC) logic or configuration files on engineering workstations. This may indicate an adversary is attempting to manipulate physical control processes.

indicators.name: Non-Standard Protocol or Command on OT Network
indicators.pattern:
title: Non-Standard Protocol or Command on OT Network
id: 8a1b9c8d-7e6f-4a5b-3c4d-2b1a9b8f5e8f
status: experimental
description: |
  Detects network connections on common OT ports (e.g., Modbus/502) originating from processes not typically associated with industrial control operations.
  This could indicate an adversary using custom tools to interact with OT devices.
references:
  - https://industrialcyber.co/news/ai-powered-cyber-threats-overwhelm-human-defenders-forcing-critical-infrastructure-operators-toward-automated-security/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-19
tags:
  - attack.t0855
level: medium
logsource:
  category: network_connection
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    DestinationPort: 502 # Modbus
    Initiated: true
  filter:
    Image|endswith: # Add legitimate HMI/SCADA/Engineering software here
      - '\Kepware.exe'
      - '\FactoryTalk.exe'
  condition: selection and not filter
indicators.description: Detects the use of non-standard protocols or commands on the OT network. This could indicate an adversary attempting to communicate with industrial controllers in an unauthorized manner.
